other_observers: Mymm Ackley; Yes-found by David Silverman. Cole Wild, Marjorie Joy, Katrina Stowasser, and Brandon Percival also saw the Warbler later in the morning. Brandon Percival photoed the bird and posted pictures on his "Flicker" website: www.flicker.com/photos/brandonsbirdphotos
duration: about 1.25 hrs during above tlime period
specific_location: On South Parkway just north of community swimming pool
description: This bird was similar in size to nearby Yellow-rumped Warbler but with a shorter tail which was dark on top with white spots below.  Head and throat were brilliant chestnut with black auriculars and a pale buffy nape. Back was gray with dark vertical stripes with one extending into and bisecting buffy nape.  Wings were darker gray with two distinct white wing bars on each wing.  Underparts were pale buff with chestnut flanks extending and melding into chestnut throat.  Legs were dark as was short slim bill.Behaviors: Actively foraged and sang in 20' tall oak trees with young leaves.Call: A thin high-pitched "tzeetz-tzeetz-tzeetza-tzeetz-tzeetz" similar to A. Redstart but higher pitched and a bit softer.Plumage: adult
